### **Description \& Requirements**

Electronic Arts creates next\-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.
Central Technology is the force multiplier, accelerating creative opportunity and progress at EA. We’re a world\-class community of technologists, innovators, strategists, and orchestrators transforming interactive entertainment. Together, we power the platforms, AI\-driven tools, live services, and infrastructure that ensure global scale, secure player experiences, and unlock bold new possibilities.

Infrastructure \& Platform Services builds and runs the technology foundation for EA’s games. Our teams deliver the live services and backend infrastructure that are used to ship, scale, and operate secure, reliable game experiences for millions of players worldwide.

**The Challenge Ahead:**

The Gameplay Services Team develops services and SDKs in collaboration with EA's game studios for matchmaking, stats and leaderboards, achievements, game replays, VOIP, and game networking. As a Backend Software Engineer, you will build scalable, distributed server\-side systems that provide functionality to our millions of customers and work on creative improvements to keep our systems at top\-notch performance. You will report to the Senior Software Engineering Manager.

**Responsibilities:**

* You will design brand new services covering all aspects from storage to application logic to management console.
* You will enhance and add features to existing systems.
* You will research and select new best\-of\-breed technologies to meet challenging requirements.
* You will optimize performance and scalability of server systems.
* You will be a part of the full product cycle for our products, from design and testing to deployment and supporting our LIVE environments and our game team customers.

**Qualifications:**

* 3\+ years hands\-on experience Java/Kotlin and Microservices
* Expertise with relational or NoSQL database
* Experience with stress\-testing and performance\-tuning and optimization
* Experience debugging and optimization experience in a multi\-threaded environment
* Experience troubleshooting and mitigating issues in live environments

**Bonus:**

* Experience with high traffic services and highly scalable, distributed systems
* Experience with scalable data storage, streaming and processing technologies such as Postgres, Cassandra, Apache Kafka, Spark
* Experience with cloud computing products such as AWS EC2, ElastiCache, and ELB
* Experience with technologies such as Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform and Redis
* Experience working within the games industry
* Knowledge of Google gRPC and protobuf

This is a hybrid remote/in\-office role.

**Pay Transparency \- North America**

**COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS**

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs). **PAY RANGES**

Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.
For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top\-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full\-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

***About Electronic Arts***
We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well\-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. jobs.title: Senior DevOps Engineer - Vancouver, BC - DESS
jobs.descriptionRaw:
**Job Title:** Senior DevOps Engineer

**Location:** Vancouver, BC

**Note:** Prior experience in **BFSI, Public Sector, or Telecom** is **non\-negotiable**.

**Position Overview:**

Seeking an experienced Senior DevOps Engineer with a strong background in BFSI, Public Sector, or Telecom environments. The ideal candidate will lead DevOps initiatives and support enterprise modernization programs.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead DevOps initiatives supporting enterprise transformation programs.
* Support modernization initiatives for core banking, payments, citizen services, OSS/BSS platforms, billing systems, charging platforms, and telecom customer platforms.
* Design and maintain enterprise CI/CD platforms.
* Lead cloud automation and Infrastructure as Code implementations.
* Support telecom infrastructure, network operations, 5G services, and enterprise digital platforms.
* Collaborate with business and technology teams supporting banking, government, and telecom operations.
* Implement security, governance, compliance, and operational controls.
* Mentor engineers and promote DevOps best practices.
* Drive monitoring, observability, and reliability improvements.
* Support continuous delivery and operational excellence initiatives.

**Required Qualifications**

* Experience as a Senior DevOps Engineer within BFSI, Public Sector, or Telecom environments.
* Experience leading DevOps transformation initiatives.
* Experience supporting banking systems, government platforms, OSS/BSS, billing, charging, or telecom environments.
* Strong expertise in CI/CD, automation, and cloud platforms.
* Experience with Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
* Strong understanding of governance, compliance, and security controls.
* Experience with Kubernetes, containers, and cloud\-native technologies.
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code and DevOps tooling.
* Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.
* Cloud or DevOps certification preferred.

**For more details reach at** **resumes@navitassols.com****.**

**About Navitas Partners, LLC:** It is a certified WBENC and one of the fastest\-growing staffing firms providing technology, professional, and consulting services across multiple industries. We offer competitive compensation and work closely with our candidates throughout the hiring process. We understand this is a partnership. You will not be blindsided, and your salary will be discussed upfront.

jobs.title: Senior Cloud Engineer - Vancouver, BC - CEHS
jobs.descriptionRaw:
**Job Title:** Senior Cloud Engineer

**Location:** Vancouver, BC

**Note:** Prior experience in **BFSI, Public Sector, or Telecom** is **non\-negotiable**.

**Position Overview:**

Seeking an experienced Senior Cloud Engineer with a strong background in BFSI, Public Sector, or Telecom environments. The ideal candidate will lead cloud engineering initiatives and support enterprise transformation programs.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead cloud engineering initiatives across BFSI, Public Sector, and Telecom environments.
* Support cloud modernization initiatives for core banking, payments, citizen services, OSS/BSS platforms, billing systems, and telecom customer platforms.
* Build and maintain cloud infrastructure supporting telecom network operations, 5G platforms, and edge computing environments.
* Provide technical leadership for enterprise cloud transformation programs.
* Collaborate with business and technology teams supporting banking, government, and telecom operations.
* Implement cloud security, governance, compliance, and operational controls.
* Mentor junior engineers and support cloud engineering best practices.
* Drive automation, monitoring, disaster recovery, and operational resilience initiatives.
* Optimize cloud performance, scalability, and cost management.
* Ensure cloud environments meet regulatory, privacy, and security requirements.

**Required Qualifications**

* Experience as a Senior Cloud Engineer within BFSI, Public Sector, or Telecom environments is required.
* Experience supporting cloud platforms for banking systems, government applications, OSS/BSS, billing, charging, or telecom customer platforms.
* Experience leading cloud transformation and modernization initiatives.
* Strong knowledge of Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
* Understanding of governance, compliance, regulatory, and security requirements.
* Experience supporting enterprise\-scale cloud environments.
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code and automation tools.
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices.
* Strong leadership, mentoring, and stakeholder management skills.
* Cloud certification preferred.

**For more details reach at** **resumes@navitassols.com****.**

**About Navitas Partners, LLC:** It is a certified WBENC and one of the fastest\-growing staffing firms providing technology, professional, and consulting services across multiple industries. We offer competitive compensation and work closely with our candidates throughout the hiring process. We understand this is a partnership. You will not be blindsided, and your salary will be discussed upfront.

jobs.title: IBM i / AS400 Developer – BFSI
jobs.descriptionRaw:
**IBM i / AS400 Developer – BFSI**
==================================

**Location:** Vancouver, BC

**About the Role**
------------------

We are seeking an IBM i / AS400 Developer to support enterprise applications running on IBM Power Systems within a BFSI environment.

**IMPORTANT NOTE – BFSI EXPERIENCE REQUIRED**
---------------------------------------------

**Candidates must have experience supporting banking, insurance, lending, payment processing, or other financial services applications running on IBM i / AS400 platforms.**

**Candidates without BFSI industry experience will not be considered.**

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Develop and maintain RPG, RPGLE, CL, and DB2 applications.
* Support enterprise IBM i environments.
* Perform system enhancements and maintenance.
* Troubleshoot production issues.
* Participate in modernization initiatives.
* Work with business stakeholders to deliver solutions.

**Required Qualifications**
---------------------------

* 5\+ years of IBM i / AS400 development experience.
* Strong RPG/RPGLE programming experience.
* Experience with DB2 for i.
* Experience supporting financial applications.

*For more details reach at* ***resumes@navitassols.com***

jobs.title: ServiceNow Integration Developer / Consultant- SNIDCBCND
jobs.descriptionRaw:
**ServiceNow Integration Developer / Consultant**
=================================================

**Job Title:** ServiceNow Integration Developer / Consultant

**Location:** GTA, Ontario, Canada

**Employment Type:** Contract / Full\-Time

### **Position Overview**

We are seeking a ServiceNow Integration Developer/Consultant to design, develop, and maintain integrations between ServiceNow and enterprise applications across complex environments.

### **Responsibilities**

* Design and develop ServiceNow integrations with internal and external systems.
* Build REST, SOAP, and API\-based integrations.
* Configure IntegrationHub spokes and data synchronization processes.
* Troubleshoot integration issues and optimize performance.
* Develop technical documentation and integration architecture designs.

### **Required Skills**

* ServiceNow IntegrationHub
* REST APIs
* SOAP Web Services
* JSON/XML
* MID Server
* JavaScript
* Web Services Development
* Azure, AWS, Salesforce, SAP, Workday integrations

### **Qualifications**

* 5\+ years of ServiceNow integration experience
* CSA and CAD Certifications preferred

### **Mandatory Requirement**

**Candidates must have prior BFSI (Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance) industry experience.**

**For more details reach at resumes@navitassols.com**

jobs.title: Java Developer – Telecom Applications - SJCAN
jobs.descriptionRaw:
**Job Title:** Java Developer – Telecom Applications

**Domain:** Telecommunications (OSS/BSS, Network Systems)

**Position Overview**

We are seeking a Java Developer to build and enhance backend systems supporting telecom operations, including OSS/BSS platforms, billing systems, and network management solutions.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Develop scalable backend applications using Java (Spring/Spring Boot)
* Build microservices for OSS/BSS platforms (order management, provisioning, billing)
* Integrate with network elements and telecom protocols (where applicable)
* Support real\-time processing for charging and billing systems
* Ensure high availability and performance for telecom\-grade systems

**Required Skills**

* Strong Java, Spring Boot, REST APIs
* Experience with microservices architecture
* Knowledge of OSS/BSS systems and telecom workflows
* Experience with high\-throughput, real\-time systems
* SQL/NoSQL databases

**Preferred**

* Exposure to 5G core systems or network orchestration
* Experience with messaging systems (Kafka, RabbitMQ)

*For more details reach at* ***resumes@navitassols.com****.*

**About Navitas Partners, LLC:** *It is a certified WBENC and one of the fastest\-growing healthcare staffing firms in the US providing Medical, Clinical and Non\-Clinical services to numerous hospitals. We offer the most competitive pay for every position we cater. We understand this is a partnership. You will not be blindsided, and your salary will be discussed upfront.*
